evidence: "All-Star Superman is probably one of the five greatest Superman stories of all time..."
"It feels like two movies that are kind of smashed together."
"The problem is that the heavy hand of DC corporate seems to have forced changes that conform to their 'New 52' status quo."
"It is impossible not to compare it to the Raimi films... Unfortunately, those comparisons are mostly unfavorable."
"The action is fun and well-thought out, if a bit redundant."
"The visual effects are a decade more advanced in the later film."
"The performances are all very strong. Every actor who has tackled Superman since Christopher Reeve has compared unfavorably."
"Sam Raimi just has a better feel for the slightly unhinged tone of the material."
"The action is fun and well-thought out, if a bit redundant."
"The characters are affected by the events of prior episodes and react in unexpected ways."
"There is real weight to the Harry Osborn arc, despite it getting a short shrift in terms of screen time."
"The idea of forcing Superman to choose between being with the woman he loves and serving the world is an excellent."

summary: The user enjoys superhero and comedy genres, showing a preference for high-quality productions with strong emotional arcs. They have mixed feelings about narrative complexity and pacing, indicating a nuanced taste in storytelling.
